name: 脚手架与基础设施
residualFeedback:
Spec is exceptionally thorough and build-ready. Every FR (1-33), every EC (1-27), every NFR, and all 13 OQs map to a concrete acceptance criterion, and the large majority also have a dedicated test case. The ConfigEnvironmentDto is a faithful 1:1 reproduction of FR-10 (all 28 vars, correct defaults, secret no-default policy, JWT-distinct and GITHUB_ENCRYPTION_KEY-exactly-32 enforced). The users-collection ownership decision and the legacy-plaintext-githubToken migration note are good proactive architectural calls that prevent a real auth/scaffold circular dependency and a real security regression respectively. Consensus = TRUE.

The six coverageGaps above are tightening items (5 missing dedicated tests + 1 clarification note), not blockers — each item is already covered by an acceptance criterion, so an engineer could build to the spec today and satisfy my requirements. Recommend the Architect add the listed TCs before handing to engineering, because they lock down behaviors (helmet headers on the wire, logging field set, .env.example completeness, preflight-never-401, raw-/health-in-boot-order) that are easy to silently regress later and are cheap to test.

Two small extras worth a note in the spec, not gaps:
- PAYLOAD_MAX_DEPTH depth-check inherently requires parsing the body, so 'rejected before the body is fully parsed' (FR-14) is technically unachievable for the depth case; the spec's DepthLimitMiddleware (walk parsed body, short-circuit before controller) is the correct achievable interpretation. Suggest rewording FR-14 to 'rejected before hand-off to controllers' to match reality.
- Multipart-upload exemption from the JSON body limit (noted in DEPTH/SIZE DoS securityNote) is a cross-domain handoff; fine to leave to the upload domain, but worth a one-line pointer in the body-limit acceptance so the engineer does not accidentally apply the JSON cap to multipart routes.

name: Auth 模块
residualFeedback:
Coverage is complete — every FR, NFR, EC, resolved OQ, and all six last-mile gaps have a matching endpoint/criterion/test. Consensus=true, no architectural rework needed. Three non-blocking refinements for the implementing engineer (do not block the build):

1. TC-REFRESH-FAMILY-EXPIRED leaves the response code ambiguous ("401 TOKEN_REVOKED/TOKEN_EXPIRED"). Pin it to ONE code (TOKEN_REVOKED is recommended, since family revocation is the semantically distinguishing action and matches the replay path) so the test asserts a single value rather than an OR.

2. FR-6/EC-1 require whole-family revocation when "a tokenHash not in the DB" is presented. For an unknown hash there is no doc and therefore no familyId to revoke — the updateMany({familyId}) is a no-op (matchedCount 0). The spec faithfully mirrors the PM requirement, but the engineer should add a one-line note that the unknown-hash branch logs AUTH_TOKEN_REVOKED with familyId=null and performs a (correct) zero-match updateMany, so reviewers don't flag it as a missing revocation.

3. NFR-3 ties the load-test spec delivery to a calendar date in the project plan ("end of Auth integration sprint"). The spec correctly enforces "green before GA" plus a binding unit-timing fallback gate, which is all it can own — just confirm the project-plan owner has that date logged so it doesn't slip silently.

All six gaps are closed exactly as the Round-1 review demanded: cookie Path pinned (Gap 1), explicit perf ACs (Gap 2), six-event-class observability made CI-enforceable (Gap 3), EC-14 partial-write proven by TC-14PW (Gap 4), EC-2 retry-cap cross-linked to Frontend (Gap 5), and >10KB pinned to 400 VALIDATION_ERROR with the 413 hedge eliminated (Gap 6). Ship it.

name: Content 模块
residualFeedback:
Spec is ACCEPTED — consensus. I cross-checked all 18 FRs, all 23 ECs, all 5 OQs (OQ13-OQ17), and all 11 NFRs against the endpoints, mongoSchema, DTOs, ACs, test cases, and securityNotes. Every requirement has a matching endpoint/criterion/test. The spec actually STRENGTHENS my requirements in two places I endorse: (1) the persisted boolean `seededAsDefault` is a cleaner mechanism for the X-Content-Source:'default' fallback (GAP-E) than my original wording, and makes the boot-vs-runtime distinction testable; (2) the explicit `diffReport:{fieldsFilled, arrayIdsGenerated, sources}` shape on POST /content/migrate is a useful addition beyond my FR10. The gapsAddressedFromPM section resolves OQ13-OQ17 exactly as I locked them (nested .strict() at every depth; sparse changed-subset audit; socialLinks 50-key/emptiness rules; GAP = absent|''|[]; default-source only at boot).

Minor nits for the Engineer (do NOT block, but address during implementation):
1. PER-IP THROTTLE TEST GAP: AC20 requires both 60/min-per-userId AND 30/min-per-IP, but the test suite only has a per-user 429 case ('Throttle 429 per user'). Add a dedicated test for the 31st-per-IP 429 (e.g. many users behind one IP, or anonymous-ish mutation attempts). The rule is in AC20; the test is missing.
2. CONFLICT(409) AUDIT DISPOSITION: AC16 explicitly excludes 401/429/VERSION_REQUIRED from writing an audit row (they fail before validation), but is silent on CONFLICT (stale If-Match). By the 'reaches validation' rule, CONFLICT should also NOT write an audit row (the version check precedes the Zod/range checks). Add one line to AC16 making this explicit so the Engineer does not over-log 409s.
3. OPERATOR ROLE LABEL: FR10 says POST /content/migrate is 'operator-only'; the spec endpoint says 'operator/admin'. If there is a real role distinction in the Auth domain, pin which role(s) may call migrate. If there is only one admin role in practice, align the wording to a single term to avoid the Engineer guessing.
4. DEFAULT-IN-CACHE OVERLAP: If boot seeds the default doc (seededAsDefault:true) and it gets cached in Redis key content:public:cache, then Mongo goes down at runtime, GET would serve it as X-Content-Source:'stale' (per the runtime Mongo-down rule) even though the underlying doc is the default. Decide and document: should the stale-cached doc carry seededAsDefault and surface X-Content-Source:'default' in that case, or is 'stale' acceptable? Pick one and add a sentence to EC12/FR18.
5. DRAFT-PRESERVATION ACROSS REFRESH: FR17 requires the in-memory admin draft to be preserved across the single 401-refresh+retry. securityNotes mentions the 1-retry-no-loop contract but not draft preservation. This is a frontend concern but please surface it as an explicit frontend AC so it is not lost (it is the difference between an admin losing vs keeping an in-progress edit on token expiry).

NFR11 (duplicate-doc guard): AC26 covers detection + alert; consider stating whether a stray duplicate is auto-removed or merely logged for operator action — current wording ('alert is logged, only canonical returned') implies log-only, which is fine, just confirm that is intended.

name: Posts 模块
residualFeedback:
Overall the spec is high quality and faithfully operationalizes the resolved PM decisions (Q-RESOLVED-A through D are each wired into an AC + TC, which is exactly what was asked). The forbidden-key allow-list, sync $set allow-list, upsert-filter isolation (source:{$in:['github',null]}), Redis lock -> 409 SYNC_RUNNING, fail-closed encryption, ETag/304, partial-merge PUT=PATCH alias, promotion rule, durable-delete blocklist, and PATH_COLLISION_AMONG_DOCS detect-and-skip policy are all present with matching acceptance criteria and tests. NFR coverage is good.

Actionable items for the Architect, in priority order:

1. GAP-1 (429 throttle): add AC-38 + a TC asserting the 101st-in-a-minute write returns 429 with a numeric Retry-After header. The status code is listed but never asserted; this is the kind of gap that ships a missing throttle.

2. GAP-2 (MANUAL_OVERRIDE contradiction): this is a genuine spec inconsistency inherited from the PM requirements. FR-12 lists MANUAL_OVERRIDE as a conflicts[] reason while FR-8(b)/EC-6 route manual-override skips into skippedManual only. The Architect should not paper over this — escalate back to PM to pick: (a) emit MANUAL_OVERRIDE in conflicts[] AND increment skippedManual, or (b) mark MANUAL_OVERRIDE reserved/unused in v1 (parallel to MARKDOWN_IMAGE_BLOCKED). Then add an AC+TC for the chosen behavior. I lean (b) since skippedManual already signals it and conflicts[] should mean 'something failed/needs triage,' but PM decides.

3. GAP-3 (sync-side readTime): add two sync-path TCs (omitted -> auto-compute CJK-aware; provided -> verbatim). FR-10 explicitly extends to sync but only the create path is tested today.

4. GAP-4 (public list ignores Authorization header): strengthen AC-1's test to send an expired/garbage Bearer token and assert 200. This is the direct regression guard for the 'public GET never reads Authorization' contract.

5. GAP-5/GAP-6/GAP-7 are low-effort additions (assert 201 on create TCs; add negative limit/page cases; add a unicode-githubPath blocklist DELETE TC).

One nit on consistency, not a gap: the ETag formula is given two slightly different ways (FR-3 'hash of content+updatedAt' vs spec 'sha256(content+|+updatedAt).slice(0,32)'). The slice(0,32) is more precise and fine; just confirm both AC-9 and the responseShape use the identical formula so the test isn't ambiguous about length.

Once GAP-1, GAP-2 (after PM resolution), and GAP-3 are addressed with ACs+TCs, and the low-effort items are swept, I will mark consensus.

name: Works 模块
residualFeedback:
The spec is strong and buildable. All 17 functional requirements, all 16 edge cases, all user stories, and all open questions are covered by endpoints, schema, ACs, and TCs — consensus holds. The route rename from PM's /works/suppressed to /works/suppressions is ratified (PM's own open question proposed it) and the literal-before-param ordering rule + AC29/TC25 correctly disambiguate it from /works/:id. The four-index single migration (AC33), the -g<6-char-nanoid> disambiguation with 3x retry-then-500, the 413-vs-400-vs-401 precedence ordering, the coverOverride URL|null model, and the MAX_FEATURED_WORKS env parsing are all crisp. Field-ownership matrix (sync $set only sync-owned; admin-owned preserved; coverOverride structurally forbidden in sync) is correct and well-tested by TC19.

Before handing to an engineer, close the six verification gaps above. The five most important, in priority order:

1. TOMBSTONE ATOMICITY (blocking decision). AC18 claims cross-collection atomicity ('IN THE SAME operation') that MongoDB single-doc writes cannot provide. The Architect MUST either mandate session.withTransaction across works.deleteOne + githubsettings.$addToSet, or explicitly downgrade to 'best-effort with documented crash window' (consistent with the MAX_FEATURED_WORKS treatment). Pick one and write the AC to match — an engineer will otherwise guess, and a crash between the two writes silently breaks the 'deletes are trustworthy' user story (the central guarantee of the domain).

2. NFR7 structured-log AC. Add a verification criterion for write-side structured logs; today only the error-response correlationId is testable.

3. SyncBatchResult shape AC + TC. The DTO is defined but never asserted; add a deep-equal test on a mixed batch (including at least one suppressed-skip AND one parse-error-skip to exercise the skippedDetails reason discriminant).

4. FR10 INSERT-seeding TC. Currently only UPDATE-preservation is tested; add a fresh-INSERT test asserting all frontmatter fields seed and coverOverride===null, featured===false.

5. Re-suppress re-seeding TC (FR9/EC5 reversibility). Verify admin-owned fields re-seed from frontmatter after a delete→un-suppress→re-sync cycle, not just that the doc reappears.

Minor note: the spec's githubsettings singleton _id is the literal string 'singleton' (good — deterministic cross-domain upsert), but no AC verifies that Works never reads/writes githubToken/githubRepo on that shared doc (only securityNotes mention it). Consider an AC asserting 'no Works-module code path touches githubToken/githubRepo' so the boundary is testable, not just prose.

Address these and the spec is ready for implementation.

name: Upload 模块
residualFeedback: Strong, build-ready spec. Every functional requirement (1-14), every edge case (1-16), and all 11 open questions are addressed with matching endpoints, acceptance criteria, and test cases. Specific strengths: (1) Defense-in-depth ordering in securityNotes is explicit and correct (no-file -> size cap -> MIME allowlist -> magic-number -> sharp decode -> pixel budget -> re-encode metadata strip -> post-encode cap -> OSS PUT). (2) The ImageProcessorPort / ObjectStoragePort abstraction satisfies the maintainability NFR and makes the retry/exists/migration contracts testable. (3) Resolving the public-read question to bucket-level policy + OSS_PUBLIC_BASE_URL env for CDN/CSP is clean. (4) Choosing BOTH rewrite-at-migration (primary) AND catch-all redirect (safety net) for backward compat exceeds the PM minimum and matches the recommendation. (5) The 50MP pre-decode pixel budget PLUS 8MB post-encode cap is the right two-layer DoS defense and goes beyond the PM's open question. (6) uuid8 in the object key cleanly resolves same-ms collisions. (7) GET /api/v1/uploads audit endpoint is a valuable addition that closes open Q10 well. Actionable tightening (all minor, non-blocking): add the dedicated refresh-Origin-mismatch test, one dual-extension assertion, an OSS HealthIndicator contract test, and clarify whether the global login rate limit covers the upload path. No structural changes needed before implementation can begin.

name: GitHub 同步模块
residualFeedback:
Overall this is an exceptionally strong spec — I confirm consensus. Every FR (1-23), every EC (1-25), every NFR (1-13), and all 17 open questions (Q1-Q17) have at least one matching acceptance criterion, and the PINNED items (rename disambiguation, post-HMAC malformed JSON, per-file timeout, webhook-not-configured, PAT redaction, compliance-doc location, lockedFields cross-domain) each have a dedicated AC AND a dedicated test case. The endpoint shapes, Mongo schema (with cross-domain contract clearly fenced off), DTOs, and dependsOnDomains declarations are all buildable. The lockedFields cross-domain delegation (Q15) is handled correctly: unit test owned here, integration test contractually owed by Posts/Works.

The gaps I flagged are TEST-CASE gaps and a few small ambiguities, not criterion gaps — they will not block an Engineer from starting, but they WILL cause rework or under-tested security paths if not closed first. Priority order for the Architect:

1. (Highest) Add the image-rewriting test matrix (FR19) and the webhook synchronous-throw test (EC23) — both are pure unit-testable, security/correctness-relevant, and currently rely on prose ACs only.
2. Clarify the /sync 500 vs 200-success:false boundary and define or remove 'bootstrap_error' — an Engineer will otherwise invent behavior.
3. Pin the webhook ordering (HMAC -> parse -> repo; HMAC before dedupe) explicitly in AC12/AC13.
4. Add the webhook+decrypt-failure combination AC/test — it is the one gap in the otherwise-exhaustive decrypt fail-closed coverage.
5. Pin the readTime word-count definition for CJK (critical for this Chinese-content site; 'whitespace-token count' under-counts Chinese text and will produce readTime='1分钟' for almost every post).
6. Add the cheap static tests for AC32 (compliance doc) and AC28 (no process.env) so NFR13/NFR10 actually ship.

One last note: the spec uses error.code 'unauthorized' for webhook missing/invalid signature while my FR4 did not mandate a code — that is acceptable, just confirm the admin-facing endpoints also use a consistent 401 code (currently they return 401 with no code listed in statusCodes; consider 'unauthorized' or 'auth_required' for envelope consistency).

name: 前端重构
residualFeedback:
Spec is strong and buildable. Every FR and EC has a matching endpoint, AC, and test case — consensus reached. Concrete notes for the Architect:

1. Add an explicit NFR-7 AC + TC: 'MarkdownContent renders both jsdelivr CDN URLs (synced) and OSS URLs (uploaded) verbatim; no URL-rewriting/transformation code exists in MarkdownContent or its callers. grep -RIn "cdn.jsdelivr|rewriteUrl|transformUrl frontend/components" returns only the sync-endpoint reference.' This is the one requirement a careful engineer could silently break.

2. Reconcile FR-7's 'merged' language with Q-9's 'about.json dropped' resolution. They say opposite things on the surface. Update FR-7 to: 'content.json.about is canonical; about.json is deleted and none of its fields are migrated.'

3. For EC-4/TC-EC-4, separate the two failure modes: (a) 403 = genuine permission/role denial -> no refresh, show permission error; (b) 404 = deleted record -> notFound() for RSC / 'not found' banner for client. The current TC blends them under 403, which leaves the deleted-record-via-api-client path under-specified.

4. Typos to fix before handing to an engineer: 'PREVIEW_BUILD_FALLLOW_JSON' (should be FALLBACK) in the Q-8 resolution, and the env var name appears only in prose — consider adding it to the env list in dependsOnDomains/infra-deploy so it isn't lost.

5. Strong points worth keeping: the coalesced-refresh AC (AC-4/AC-17) is verifiable by counting refresh network calls; the grep-based ACs (AC-1/AC-2/AC-3/AC-6/AC-8/AC-10/AC-11/AC-23/AC-26) are concrete and automatable; the version/If-Match 409 contract cleanly resolves EC-10 with last-write-wins as the documented default. The Origin-validation CSRF defense is correctly scoped as a backend dependency (AC-20).
